source.title: Gas Well Blowout and Fire at Pryor Trust Well 1H-9

event.top_event: Gas well blowout and rig fire
actions_taken: Attempt to close blowout preventer after fire started
event.summary: On January 22, 2018, a blowout and rig fire occurred at Pryor Trust Well 1H-9 in Pittsburg County, Oklahoma. The fire killed five workers inside the driller's cabin from thermal burn injuries and smoke/soot inhalation. The blowout occurred approximately 3.5 hours after tripping drill pipe out of the well. Root causes included failure of the primary barrier (hydrostatic pressure from drilling mud) and secondary barrier (human detection of influx and BOP activation). Contributing factors included underbalanced operations performed without proper planning or equipment, inadequate training on electronic trip sheets, equipment misalignment causing data interpretation errors, failure to identify surface pressure, miscalculated weighted pill, deactivated alarm system, lack of flow checks, inadequate driller training in influx detection, and ineffective safety management system. The BOP failed to close after the fire because hydraulic control hoses burned and leaked, depleting accumulator pressure.
recommendations: OSHA should establish drilling-specific regulatory standards for onshore drilling safety
API should develop industry standards for alarm management in drilling operations
API should develop industry guidance on tripping procedures for underbalanced operations
Patterson-UTI should implement effective monitoring of policy implementation including flow checks
Patterson-UTI should test drillers' influx detection skills through simulated pit gains
Patterson-UTI should improve driller training on electronic trip sheets
Red Mountain Operating should specify required barriers during operations and response procedures
IADC should develop tripping guidance for underbalanced operations
Pason Systems should improve alarm system design for different operating states
NOV should improve BOP design and hydraulic line protection
State of Oklahoma should establish drilling safety regulations
key_phrases: Underbalanced Operations Performed Without Proper Planning, Procedures, or Needed Equipment
Signs of Influx Either Not Identified or Inadequately Responded To
BOP Could Not Close Due to Burned Hydraulic Hoses
Lack of Safety Requirements by Regulation

threats.name: Loss of hydrostatic pressure barrier during underbalanced operations
Gas influx into wellbore during tripping operations
Failure to detect gas influx at surface
Swabbing effect reducing wellbore pressure during pipe removal
Inadequate mud weight for well conditions

consequences.name: Fatalities from thermal burns and smoke inhalation

controls.name: Hydrostatic pressure from drilling mud column
controls.barrier_role: Primary barrier to prevent gas influx
controls.lod_basis: Mud density and column height maintained above formation pressure

supporting_text: Underbalanced drilling was performed without needed planning, equipment, skills, or procedures, thus nullifying the planned primary barrier to prevent gas influx
Tripping was performed out of the underbalanced well, which allowed a large amount of gas to enter the well
A weighted pill intended to overbalance the well was apparently miscalculated. After pill placement, the well was still underbalanced

controls.name: Human detection of gas influx and BOP activation
controls.barrier_role: Secondary barrier to detect and contain influx
controls.lod_basis: Driller and company man monitoring for pit gains and flow indications

supporting_text: The driller was not effectively trained in using a new electronic trip sheet, which is used to help monitor for gas influx
Equipment was aligned differently than normal during the tripping operation, leading to confusion in interpreting the well data which caused rig workers to miss indications of the gas influx
Both the day and night driller chose to turn off the entire alarm system, contributing to both drillers missing critical indications of the gas influx
Key flow checks to determine if the well was flowing were not performed before the incident

controls.name: Alarm system for monitoring well conditions
controls.lod_basis: Automated alerts for pit gains and flow anomalies

supporting_text: Both the day and night driller chose to turn off the entire alarm system, contributing to both drillers missing critical indications of the gas influx and imminent blowout

The alarm system also was not effectively designed to alert personnel to hazardous conditions during different operating states (e.g., drilling, tripping, circulating, and surface operations) and would have sounded excessive non-critical alarms during the 14 hours leading to the blowout, which likely led to the drillers choosing to turn off the alarm system

controls.name: Flow checks to detect influx
controls.lod_basis: Periodic halting of operations to monitor well flow

supporting_text: Key flow checks to determine if the well was flowing were not performed before the incident
Drilling rig workers performed very few of the company-required flow checks during the drilling of well 1H-9 and the previous well
The drilling contractor did not effectively monitor the implementation rate of its flow check policy

controls.name: Blowout preventer (BOP) closure
controls.barrier_role: Containment of well pressure and influx
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical closure of rams and annular preventer

supporting_text: The blowout preventer also failed to close when its activation was attempted after the fire started

The CSB determined the BOP did not function likely because the control hoses that supplied hydraulic fluid to the BOP to function the rams had burned in the fire and leaked the hydraulic control fluid, soon depleting the accumulator stored pressure to the point the blowout preventer could not be closed

controls.name: Weighted pill to overbalance well
controls.barrier_role: Temporary pressure increase to prevent influx
controls.lod_basis: Denser mud column to increase hydrostatic pressure

supporting_text: A weighted pill intended to overbalance the well was apparently miscalculated. After pill placement, the well was still underbalanced

controls.name: Electronic trip sheet for monitoring during pipe removal
controls.barrier_role: Data tracking and influx detection aid
controls.lod_basis: Electronic recording of mud volumes and pressures during tripping

supporting_text: The driller was not effectively trained in using a new electronic trip sheet, which is used to help monitor for gas influx
Driller Had Difficulties with Electronic Trip Sheet

controls.name: Tripping procedure and equipment alignment
controls.barrier_role: Operational guidance to maintain well control during pipe removal
controls.lod_basis: Documented procedures for safe tripping operations

supporting_text: Equipment was aligned differently than normal during the tripping operation, leading to confusion in interpreting the well data which caused rig workers to miss indications of the gas influx
Tripping Procedure Did Not Specify Equipment Alignment
Lack of Detail in Procedures

controls.name: Management of Change (MOC) process
controls.barrier_role: Control of operational deviations and safety assessment
controls.lod_basis: Formal process to evaluate and approve changes to drilling plan

supporting_text: Management of Change Ineffective for Operational Changes

The operating company did not specify the barriers required during operations, or how to respond if a barrier was lost. This contributed to the performance of underbalanced operations that the drilling rig and its crew were not equipped or trained to perform

controls.name: Driller training and competency assessment for influx detection
controls.barrier_role: Human capability to recognize and respond to influx
controls.lod_basis: Training and testing of driller skills in detecting pit gains and flow anomalies

supporting_text: The drilling contractor did not test its drillers' abilities in detecting indications of gas influx through, for example, simulated pit gains
Drills Did Not Test Driller Influx Detection Skills

controls.name: Barrier specification and loss response procedures
controls.barrier_role: Operational planning to ensure adequate barriers and response protocols
controls.lod_basis: Well plan specifying required barriers and contingency actions

supporting_text: The operating company did not specify the barriers required during operations, or how to respond if a barrier was lost

controls.name: Driller's cabin design and escape routes
controls.barrier_role: Protection and safe egress for personnel
controls.lod_basis: Structural design and emergency exit provisions

supporting_text: The victims had no safe escape route from the driller's cabin (dog house) once the drilling mud and gas ignited. The workers were effectively trapped once the fire started
Driller's Cabin Design

controls.name: Surface pressure identification before BOP operations
controls.barrier_role: Detection of well pressurization indicating influx
controls.lod_basis: Monitoring and recording of surface pressure readings

supporting_text: Surface pressure was not identified two separate times before opening the BOP during operations before the blowout, when there was evidently pressure at the surface of the well. This non-identification of surface pressure contributed to the gas influx not being identified

controls.name: Safety management system and policy implementation monitoring
controls.barrier_role: Oversight of operational safety practices and compliance

supporting_text: Patterson Did Not Effectively Monitor the Implementation of its Policies
The safety management system in place was not effective for managing safe rig operations

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
